evals.name: no-implicit-any
evals.prompt: Apply typescript-best-practices to this function: function parse(raw) { return JSON.parse(raw); }
evals.expected_output: Output types the parameter and return, avoiding implicit any, and prefers a narrow parsed type over unknown.
assertions: The output provides an explicit return type
The output does not use `any` as the parsed result type

evals.name: exhaustive-union
evals.prompt: Apply typescript-best-practices to a switch over a string-literal union Status = 'open' | 'closed' | 'pending' that currently has no default.
evals.expected_output: Output uses a never-based exhaustiveness check so adding a new union member is a compile error.
assertions: The output adds an exhaustiveness check (assertNever / never assignment)
The output flags the missing case rather than silently defaulting
Adding a new union member would produce a type error
